rationale: Melumad et al. (2019) is cited in the introduction, contribution section, and Table 1 to define an existing line of work on mobile versus nonmobile differences in UGC content. The citing paper explicitly contrasts this by studying how a mobile-origin cue affects review evaluation and purchase intentions while holding review text constant. This use is more than a passing mention because it helps motivate the research gap and position the paper's contribution. However, the citing paper does not directly reuse or contest Melumad et al.'s specific mechanism or design.
relation_description: The citing paper substantively positions itself against Melumad et al. (2019) by moving from production-side effects of mobile on UGC content (e.g., emotionality) to receiver-side effects of seeing a mobile label on otherwise identical reviews. The cited paper functions as a key contrastive anchor in the conceptual framing.
